2133 // General case, the type indexed by Idxs is a struct
2134 for (unsigned i = 0, e = STy->getNumElements(); i != e; ++i) {
2135 // Process each struct element recursively
2136 Idxs.push_back(i);
2137 Value *PrevTo = To;
2138 To = BuildSubAggregate(From, To, STy->getElementType(i), Idxs, IdxSkip,
2139 InsertBefore);
2140 Idxs.pop_back();
2141 if (!To) {
2142 // Couldn't find any inserted value for this index? Cleanup
2143 while (PrevTo != OrigTo) {
2144 InsertValueInst* Del = cast<InsertValueInst>(PrevTo);
2145 PrevTo = Del->getAggregateOperand();
2146 Del->eraseFromParent();
2147 }
2148 // Stop processing elements
2149 break;
2150 }
2151 }
2152 // If we successfully found a value for each of our subaggregates
2153 if (To)
2154 return To;
2155 }
2156 // Base case, the type indexed by SourceIdxs is not a struct, or not all of
2157 // the struct's elements had a value that was inserted directly. In the latter
2158 // case, perhaps we can't determine each of the subelements individually, but
2159 // we might be able to find the complete struct somewhere.
2160
2161 // Find the value that is at that particular spot
2162 Value *V = FindInsertedValue(From, Idxs);
2163
2164 if (!V)
2165 return nullptr;
2166
2167 // Insert the value in the new (sub) aggregrate
2168 return llvm::InsertValueInst::Create(To, V, makeArrayRef(Idxs).slice(IdxSkip),
2169 "tmp", InsertBefore);
2170}
2171
2172// This helper takes a nested struct and extracts a part of it (which is again a
2173// struct) into a new value. For example, given the struct:
2174// { a, { b, { c, d }, e } }
2175// and the indices "1, 1" this returns
2176// { c, d }.
2177//
2178// It does this by inserting an insertvalue for each element in the resulting
2179// struct, as opposed to just inserting a single struct. This will only work if
2180// each of the elements of the substruct are known (ie, inserted into From by an
2181// insertvalue instruction somewhere).
2182//
2183// All inserted insertvalue instructions are inserted before InsertBefore
2184static Value *BuildSubAggregate(Value *From, ArrayRef<unsigned> idx_range,
2185 Instruction *InsertBefore) {
2186 assert(InsertBefore && "Must have someplace to insert!");
2187 Type *IndexedType = ExtractValueInst::getIndexedType(From->getType(),
2188 idx_range);
2189 Value *To = UndefValue::get(IndexedType);
2190 SmallVector<unsigned, 10> Idxs(idx_range.begin(), idx_range.end());
2191 unsigned IdxSkip = Idxs.size();
2192
2193 return BuildSubAggregate(From, To, IndexedType, Idxs, IdxSkip, InsertBefore);
2194}
2195
2196/// Given an aggregrate and an sequence of indices, see if
2197/// the scalar value indexed is already around as a register, for example if it
2198/// were inserted directly into the aggregrate.
2199///
2200/// If InsertBefore is not null, this function will duplicate (modified)
2201/// insertvalues when a part of a nested struct is extracted.
2202Value *llvm::FindInsertedValue(Value *V, ArrayRef<unsigned> idx_range,
2203 Instruction *InsertBefore) {
2204 // Nothing to index? Just return V then (this is useful at the end of our
2205 // recursion).
2206 if (idx_range.empty())
2207 return V;
2208 // We have indices, so V should have an indexable type.
2209 assert((V->getType()->isStructTy() || V->getType()->isArrayTy()) &&
2210 "Not looking at a struct or array?");
2211 assert(ExtractValueInst::getIndexedType(V->getType(), idx_range) &&
2212 "Invalid indices for type?");
2213
2214 if (Constant *C = dyn_cast<Constant>(V)) {
2215 C = C->getAggregateElement(idx_range[0]);
2216 if (!C) return nullptr;
2217 return FindInsertedValue(C, idx_range.slice(1), InsertBefore);
2218 }
2219
2220 if (InsertValueInst *I = dyn_cast<InsertValueInst>(V)) {
2221 // Loop the indices for the insertvalue instruction in parallel with the
2222 // requested indices
2223 const unsigned *req_idx = idx_range.begin();
2224 for (const unsigned *i = I->idx_begin(), *e = I->idx_end();
2225 i != e; ++i, ++req_idx) {
2226 if (req_idx == idx_range.end()) {
2227 // We can't handle this without inserting insertvalues
2228 if (!InsertBefore)
2229 return nullptr;
2230
2231 // The requested index identifies a part of a nested aggregate. Handle
2232 // this specially. For example,
2233 // %A = insertvalue { i32, {i32, i32 } } undef, i32 10, 1, 0
2234 // %B = insertvalue { i32, {i32, i32 } } %A, i32 11, 1, 1
2235 // %C = extractvalue {i32, { i32, i32 } } %B, 1
2236 // This can be changed into
2237 // %A = insertvalue {i32, i32 } undef, i32 10, 0
2238 // %C = insertvalue {i32, i32 } %A, i32 11, 1
2239 // which allows the unused 0,0 element from the nested struct to be
2240 // removed.
2241 return BuildSubAggregate(V, makeArrayRef(idx_range.begin(), req_idx),
2242 InsertBefore);
2243 }
2244
2245 // This insert value inserts something else than what we are looking for.
2246 // See if the (aggregrate) value inserted into has the value we are
2247 // looking for, then.
2248 if (*req_idx != *i)
2249 return FindInsertedValue(I->getAggregateOperand(), idx_range,
2250 InsertBefore);
2251 }
2252 // If we end up here, the indices of the insertvalue match with those
2253 // requested (though possibly only partially). Now we recursively look at
2254 // the inserted value, passing any remaining indices.
2255 return FindInsertedValue(I->getInsertedValueOperand(),
2256 makeArrayRef(req_idx, idx_range.end()),
2257 InsertBefore);
2258 }
2259
2260 if (ExtractValueInst *I = dyn_cast<ExtractValueInst>(V)) {
2261 // If we're extracting a value from an aggregrate that was extracted from
2262 // something else, we can extract from that something else directly instead.
2263 // However, we will need to chain I's indices with the requested indices.
2264
2265 // Calculate the number of indices required
2266 unsigned size = I->getNumIndices() + idx_range.size();
2267 // Allocate some space to put the new indices in
2268 SmallVector<unsigned, 5> Idxs;
2269 Idxs.reserve(size);
2270 // Add indices from the extract value instruction
2271 Idxs.append(I->idx_begin(), I->idx_end());
2272
2273 // Add requested indices
2274 Idxs.append(idx_range.begin(), idx_range.end());
2275
2276 assert(Idxs.size() == size
2277 && "Number of indices added not correct?");
2278
2279 return FindInsertedValue(I->getAggregateOperand(), Idxs, InsertBefore);
2280 }
2281 // Otherwise, we don't know (such as, extracting from a function return value
2282 // or load instruction)
2283 return nullptr;
2284}
2285
2286/// Analyze the specified pointer to see if it can be expressed as a base
2287/// pointer plus a constant offset. Return the base and offset to the caller.
2288Value *llvm::GetPointerBaseWithConstantOffset(Value *Ptr, int64_t &Offset,
2289 const DataLayout *DL) {
2290 // Without DataLayout, conservatively assume 64-bit offsets, which is
2291 // the widest we support.
2292 unsigned BitWidth = DL ? DL->getPointerTypeSizeInBits(Ptr->getType()) : 64;
2293 APInt ByteOffset(BitWidth, 0);
2294 while (1) {
2295 if (Ptr->getType()->isVectorTy())
2296 break;
2297
2298 if (GEPOperator *GEP = dyn_cast<GEPOperator>(Ptr)) {
2299 if (DL) {
2300 APInt GEPOffset(BitWidth, 0);
2301 if (!GEP->accumulateConstantOffset(*DL, GEPOffset))
2302 break;
2303
2304 ByteOffset += GEPOffset;
2305 }
2306
2307 Ptr = GEP->getPointerOperand();
2308 } else if (Operator::getOpcode(Ptr) == Instruction::BitCast ||
2309 Operator::getOpcode(Ptr) == Instruction::AddrSpaceCast) {
2310 Ptr = cast<Operator>(Ptr)->getOperand(0);
2311 } else if (GlobalAlias *GA = dyn_cast<GlobalAlias>(Ptr)) {
2312 if (GA->mayBeOverridden())
2313 break;
2314 Ptr = GA->getAliasee();
2315 } else {
2316 break;
2317 }
2318 }
2319 Offset = ByteOffset.getSExtValue();
2320 return Ptr;
2321}
2322
2323
2324/// This function computes the length of a null-terminated C string pointed to
2325/// by V. If successful, it returns true and returns the string in Str.
2326/// If unsuccessful, it returns false.
2327bool llvm::getConstantStringInfo(const Value *V, StringRef &Str,
2328 uint64_t Offset, bool TrimAtNul) {
2329 assert(V);
2330
2331 // Look through bitcast instructions and geps.
2332 V = V->stripPointerCasts();
2333
2334 // If the value is a GEP instructionor constant expression, treat it as an
2335 // offset.
2336 if (const GEPOperator *GEP = dyn_cast<GEPOperator>(V)) {
2337 // Make sure the GEP has exactly three arguments.
2338 if (GEP->getNumOperands() != 3)
2339 return false;
2340
2341 // Make sure the index-ee is a pointer to array of i8.
2342 PointerType *PT = cast<PointerType>(GEP->getOperand(0)->getType());
2343 ArrayType *AT = dyn_cast<ArrayType>(PT->getElementType());
2344 if (!AT || !AT->getElementType()->isIntegerTy(8))
2345 return false;
2346
2347 // Check to make sure that the first operand of the GEP is an integer and
2348 // has value 0 so that we are sure we're indexing into the initializer.
2349 const ConstantInt *FirstIdx = dyn_cast<ConstantInt>(GEP->getOperand(1));
2350 if (!FirstIdx || !FirstIdx->isZero())
2351 return false;
2352
2353 // If the second index isn't a ConstantInt, then this is a variable index
2354 // into the array. If this occurs, we can't say anything meaningful about
2355 // the string.
2356 uint64_t StartIdx = 0;
2357 if (const ConstantInt *CI = dyn_cast<ConstantInt>(GEP->getOperand(2)))
2358 StartIdx = CI->getZExtValue();
2359 else
2360 return false;
2361 return getConstantStringInfo(GEP->getOperand(0), Str, StartIdx+Offset);
2362 }
2363
2364 // The GEP instruction, constant or instruction, must reference a global
2365 // variable that is a constant and is initialized. The referenced constant
2366 // initializer is the array that we'll use for optimization.
2367 const GlobalVariable *GV = dyn_cast<GlobalVariable>(V);
2368 if (!GV || !GV->isConstant() || !GV->hasDefinitiveInitializer())
2369 return false;
2370
2371 // Handle the all-zeros case
2372 if (GV->getInitializer()->isNullValue()) {
2373 // This is a degenerate case. The initializer is constant zero so the
2374 // length of the string must be zero.
2375 Str = "";
2376 return true;
2377 }
2378
2379 // Must be a Constant Array
2380 const ConstantDataArray *Array =
2381 dyn_cast<ConstantDataArray>(GV->getInitializer());
2382 if (!Array || !Array->isString())
2383 return false;
2384
2385 // Get the number of elements in the array
2386 uint64_t NumElts = Array->getType()->getArrayNumElements();
2387
2388 // Start out with the entire array in the StringRef.
2389 Str = Array->getAsString();
2390
2391 if (Offset > NumElts)
2392 return false;
2393
2394 // Skip over 'offset' bytes.
2395 Str = Str.substr(Offset);
2396
2397 if (TrimAtNul) {
2398 // Trim off the \0 and anything after it. If the array is not nul
2399 // terminated, we just return the whole end of string. The client may know
2400 // some other way that the string is length-bound.
2401 Str = Str.substr(0, Str.find('\0'));
2402 }
2403 return true;
2404}
2405
2406// These next two are very similar to the above, but also look through PHI
2407// nodes.
2408// TODO: See if we can integrate these two together.
2409
2410/// If we can compute the length of the string pointed to by
2411/// the specified pointer, return 'len+1'. If we can't, return 0.
2412static uint64_t GetStringLengthH(Value *V, SmallPtrSetImpl<PHINode*> &PHIs) {
2413 // Look through noop bitcast instructions.
2414 V = V->stripPointerCasts();
2415
2416 // If this is a PHI node, there are two cases: either we have already seen it
2417 // or we haven't.
2418 if (PHINode *PN = dyn_cast<PHINode>(V)) {
2419 if (!PHIs.insert(PN).second)
2420 return ~0ULL; // already in the set.
2421
2422 // If it was new, see if all the input strings are the same length.
2423 uint64_t LenSoFar = ~0ULL;
2424 for (unsigned i = 0, e = PN->getNumIncomingValues(); i != e; ++i) {
2425 uint64_t Len = GetStringLengthH(PN->getIncomingValue(i), PHIs);
2426 if (Len == 0) return 0; // Unknown length -> unknown.
2427
2428 if (Len == ~0ULL) continue;
2429
2430 if (Len != LenSoFar && LenSoFar != ~0ULL)
2431 return 0; // Disagree -> unknown.
2432 LenSoFar = Len;
2433 }
2434
2435 // Success, all agree.
2436 return LenSoFar;
2437 }
2438
2439 // strlen(select(c,x,y)) -> strlen(x) ^ strlen(y)
2440 if (SelectInst *SI = dyn_cast<SelectInst>(V)) {
2441 uint64_t Len1 = GetStringLengthH(SI->getTrueValue(), PHIs);
2442 if (Len1 == 0) return 0;
2443 uint64_t Len2 = GetStringLengthH(SI->getFalseValue(), PHIs);
2444 if (Len2 == 0) return 0;
2445 if (Len1 == ~0ULL) return Len2;
2446 if (Len2 == ~0ULL) return Len1;
2447 if (Len1 != Len2) return 0;
2448 return Len1;
2449 }
2450
2451 // Otherwise, see if we can read the string.
2452 StringRef StrData;
2453 if (!getConstantStringInfo(V, StrData))
2454 return 0;
2455
2456 return StrData.size()+1;
2457}
2458
2459/// If we can compute the length of the string pointed to by
2460/// the specified pointer, return 'len+1'. If we can't, return 0.
2461uint64_t llvm::GetStringLength(Value *V) {
2462 if (!V->getType()->isPointerTy()) return 0;
2463
2464 SmallPtrSet<PHINode*, 32> PHIs;
2465 uint64_t Len = GetStringLengthH(V, PHIs);
2466 // If Len is ~0ULL, we had an infinite phi cycle: this is dead code, so return
2467 // an empty string as a length.
2468 return Len == ~0ULL ? 1 : Len;
2469}
2470
2471Value *
2472llvm::GetUnderlyingObject(Value *V, const DataLayout *TD, unsigned MaxLookup) {
2473 if (!V->getType()->isPointerTy())
2474 return V;
2475 for (unsigned Count = 0; MaxLookup == 0 || Count < MaxLookup; ++Count) {
2476 if (GEPOperator *GEP = dyn_cast<GEPOperator>(V)) {
2477 V = GEP->getPointerOperand();
2478 } else if (Operator::getOpcode(V) == Instruction::BitCast ||
2479 Operator::getOpcode(V) == Instruction::AddrSpaceCast) {
2480 V = cast<Operator>(V)->getOperand(0);
2481 } else if (GlobalAlias *GA = dyn_cast<GlobalAlias>(V)) {
2482 if (GA->mayBeOverridden())
2483 return V;
2484 V = GA->getAliasee();
2485 } else {
2486 // See if InstructionSimplify knows any relevant tricks.
2487 if (Instruction *I = dyn_cast<Instruction>(V))
2488 // TODO: Acquire a DominatorTree and AssumptionCache and use them.
2489 if (Value *Simplified = SimplifyInstruction(I, TD, nullptr)) {
2490 V = Simplified;
2491 continue;
2492 }
2493
2494 return V;
2495 }
2496 assert(V->getType()->isPointerTy() && "Unexpected operand type!");
2497 }
2498 return V;
2499}
2500
2501void
2502llvm::GetUnderlyingObjects(Value *V,
2503 SmallVectorImpl<Value *> &Objects,
2504 const DataLayout *TD,
2505 unsigned MaxLookup) {
2506 SmallPtrSet<Value *, 4> Visited;
2507 SmallVector<Value *, 4> Worklist;
2508 Worklist.push_back(V);
2509 do {
2510 Value *P = Worklist.pop_back_val();
2511 P = GetUnderlyingObject(P, TD, MaxLookup);
2512
2513 if (!Visited.insert(P).second)
2514 continue;
2515
2516 if (SelectInst *SI = dyn_cast<SelectInst>(P)) {
2517 Worklist.push_back(SI->getTrueValue());
2518 Worklist.push_back(SI->getFalseValue());
2519 continue;
2520 }
2521
2522 if (PHINode *PN = dyn_cast<PHINode>(P)) {
2523 for (unsigned i = 0, e = PN->getNumIncomingValues(); i != e; ++i)
2524 Worklist.push_back(PN->getIncomingValue(i));
2525 continue;
2526 }
2527
2528 Objects.push_back(P);
2529 } while (!Worklist.empty());
2530}
2531
2532/// Return true if the only users of this pointer are lifetime markers.
2533bool llvm::onlyUsedByLifetimeMarkers(const Value *V) {
2534 for (const User *U : V->users()) {
2535 const IntrinsicInst *II = dyn_cast<IntrinsicInst>(U);
2536 if (!II) return false;
2537
2538 if (II->getIntrinsicID() != Intrinsic::lifetime_start &&
2539 II->getIntrinsicID() != Intrinsic::lifetime_end)
2540 return false;
2541 }
2542 return true;
2543}
2544
2545bool llvm::isSafeToSpeculativelyExecute(const Value *V,
2546 const DataLayout *TD) {
2547 const Operator *Inst = dyn_cast<Operator>(V);
2548 if (!Inst)
2549 return false;
2550
2551 for (unsigned i = 0, e = Inst->getNumOperands(); i != e; ++i)
2552 if (Constant *C = dyn_cast<Constant>(Inst->getOperand(i)))
2553 if (C->canTrap())
2554 return false;
2555
2556 switch (Inst->getOpcode()) {
2557 default:
2558 return true;
2559 case Instruction::UDiv:
2560 case Instruction::URem: {
2561 // x / y is undefined if y == 0.
2562 const APInt *V;
2563 if (match(Inst->getOperand(1), m_APInt(V)))
2564 return *V != 0;
2565 return false;
2566 }
2567 case Instruction::SDiv:
2568 case Instruction::SRem: {
2569 // x / y is undefined if y == 0 or x == INT_MIN and y == -1
2570 const APInt *X, *Y;
2571 if (match(Inst->getOperand(1), m_APInt(Y))) {
2572 if (*Y != 0) {
2573 if (*Y == -1) {
2574 // The numerator can't be MinSignedValue if the denominator is -1.
2575 if (match(Inst->getOperand(0), m_APInt(X)))
2576 return !Y->isMinSignedValue();
2577 // The numerator *might* be MinSignedValue.
2578 return false;
2579 }
2580 // The denominator is not 0 or -1, it's safe to proceed.
2581 return true;
2582 }
2583 }
2584 return false;
2585 }
2586 case Instruction::Load: {
2587 const LoadInst *LI = cast<LoadInst>(Inst);
2588 if (!LI->isUnordered() ||
2589 // Speculative load may create a race that did not exist in the source.
2590 LI->getParent()->getParent()->hasFnAttribute(Attribute::SanitizeThread))
2591 return false;
2592 return LI->getPointerOperand()->isDereferenceablePointer(TD);
2593 }
2594 case Instruction::Call: {
2595 if (const IntrinsicInst *II = dyn_cast<IntrinsicInst>(Inst)) {
2596 switch (II->getIntrinsicID()) {
2597 // These synthetic intrinsics have no side-effects and just mark
2598 // information about their operands.
2599 // FIXME: There are other no-op synthetic instructions that potentially
2600 // should be considered at least *safe* to speculate...
2601 case Intrinsic::dbg_declare:
2602 case Intrinsic::dbg_value:
2603 return true;
2604
2605 case Intrinsic::bswap:
2606 case Intrinsic::ctlz:
2607 case Intrinsic::ctpop:
2608 case Intrinsic::cttz:
2609 case Intrinsic::objectsize:
2610 case Intrinsic::sadd_with_overflow:
2611 case Intrinsic::smul_with_overflow:
2612 case Intrinsic::ssub_with_overflow:
2613 case Intrinsic::uadd_with_overflow:
2614 case Intrinsic::umul_with_overflow:
2615 case Intrinsic::usub_with_overflow:
2616 return true;
2617 // Sqrt should be OK, since the llvm sqrt intrinsic isn't defined to set
2618 // errno like libm sqrt would.
2619 case Intrinsic::sqrt:
2620 case Intrinsic::fma:
2621 case Intrinsic::fmuladd:
2622 case Intrinsic::fabs:
2623 case Intrinsic::minnum:
2624 case Intrinsic::maxnum:
2625 return true;
2626 // TODO: some fp intrinsics are marked as having the same error handling
2627 // as libm. They're safe to speculate when they won't error.
2628 // TODO: are convert_{from,to}_fp16 safe?
2629 // TODO: can we list target-specific intrinsics here?
2630 default: break;
2631 }
2632 }
2633 return false; // The called function could have undefined behavior or
2634 // side-effects, even if marked readnone nounwind.
2635 }
2636 case Instruction::VAArg:
2637 case Instruction::Alloca:
2638 case Instruction::Invoke:
2639 case Instruction::PHI:
2640 case Instruction::Store:
2641 case Instruction::Ret:
2642 case Instruction::Br:
2643 case Instruction::IndirectBr:
2644 case Instruction::Switch:
2645 case Instruction::Unreachable:
2646 case Instruction::Fence:
2647 case Instruction::LandingPad:
2648 case Instruction::AtomicRMW:
2649 case Instruction::AtomicCmpXchg:
2650 case Instruction::Resume:
2651 return false; // Misc instructions which have effects
2652 }
2653}
2654
2655/// Return true if we know that the specified value is never null.
2656bool llvm::isKnownNonNull(const Value *V, const TargetLibraryInfo *TLI) {
2657 // Alloca never returns null, malloc might.
2658 if (isa<AllocaInst>(V)) return true;
2659
2660 // A byval, inalloca, or nonnull argument is never null.
2661 if (const Argument *A = dyn_cast<Argument>(V))
2662 return A->hasByValOrInAllocaAttr() || A->hasNonNullAttr();
2663
2664 // Global values are not null unless extern weak.
2665 if (const GlobalValue *GV = dyn_cast<GlobalValue>(V))
2666 return !GV->hasExternalWeakLinkage();
2667
2668 // A Load tagged w/nonnull metadata is never null.
2669 if (const LoadInst *LI = dyn_cast<LoadInst>(V))
2670 return LI->getMetadata(LLVMContext::MD_nonnull);
2671
2672 if (ImmutableCallSite CS = V)
2673 if (CS.isReturnNonNull())
2674 return true;
2675
2676 // operator new never returns null.
2677 if (isOperatorNewLikeFn(V, TLI, /*LookThroughBitCast=*/true))
2678 return true;
2679
2680 return false;
2681}
2682
2683OverflowResult llvm::computeOverflowForUnsignedMul(Value *LHS, Value *RHS,
2684 const DataLayout *DL,
2685 AssumptionCache *AC,
2686 const Instruction *CxtI,
2687 const DominatorTree *DT) {
2688 // Multiplying n * m significant bits yields a result of n + m significant
2689 // bits. If the total number of significant bits does not exceed the
2690 // result bit width (minus 1), there is no overflow.
2691 // This means if we have enough leading zero bits in the operands
2692 // we can guarantee that the result does not overflow.
2693 // Ref: "Hacker's Delight" by Henry Warren
2694 unsigned BitWidth = LHS->getType()->getScalarSizeInBits();
2695 APInt LHSKnownZero(BitWidth, 0);
2696 APInt LHSKnownOne(BitWidth, 0);
2697 APInt RHSKnownZero(BitWidth, 0);
2698 APInt RHSKnownOne(BitWidth, 0);
2699 computeKnownBits(LHS, LHSKnownZero, LHSKnownOne, DL, /*Depth=*/0, AC, CxtI,
2700 DT);
2701 computeKnownBits(RHS, RHSKnownZero, RHSKnownOne, DL, /*Depth=*/0, AC, CxtI,
2702 DT);
2703 // Note that underestimating the number of zero bits gives a more
2704 // conservative answer.
2705 unsigned ZeroBits = LHSKnownZero.countLeadingOnes() +
2706 RHSKnownZero.countLeadingOnes();
2707 // First handle the easy case: if we have enough zero bits there's
2708 // definitely no overflow.
2709 if (ZeroBits >= BitWidth)
2710 return OverflowResult::NeverOverflows;
2711
2712 // Get the largest possible values for each operand.
2713 APInt LHSMax = ~LHSKnownZero;
2714 APInt RHSMax = ~RHSKnownZero;
2715
2716 // We know the multiply operation doesn't overflow if the maximum values for
2717 // each operand will not overflow after we multiply them together.
2718 bool MaxOverflow;
2719 LHSMax.umul_ov(RHSMax, MaxOverflow);
2720 if (!MaxOverflow)
2721 return OverflowResult::NeverOverflows;
2722
2723 // We know it always overflows if multiplying the smallest possible values for
2724 // the operands also results in overflow.
2725 bool MinOverflow;
2726 LHSKnownOne.umul_ov(RHSKnownOne, MinOverflow);
2727 if (MinOverflow)
2728 return OverflowResult::AlwaysOverflows;
2729
2730 return OverflowResult::MayOverflow;
2731}
2732
2733OverflowResult llvm::computeOverflowForUnsignedAdd(Value *LHS, Value *RHS,
2734 const DataLayout *DL,
2735 AssumptionCache *AC,
2736 const Instruction *CxtI,
2737 const DominatorTree *DT) {
2738 bool LHSKnownNonNegative, LHSKnownNegative;
2739 ComputeSignBit(LHS, LHSKnownNonNegative, LHSKnownNegative, DL, /*Depth=*/0,
2740 AC, CxtI, DT);
2741 if (LHSKnownNonNegative || LHSKnownNegative) {
2742 bool RHSKnownNonNegative, RHSKnownNegative;
2743 ComputeSignBit(RHS, RHSKnownNonNegative, RHSKnownNegative, DL, /*Depth=*/0,
2744 AC, CxtI, DT);
2745
2746 if (LHSKnownNegative && RHSKnownNegative) {
2747 // The sign bit is set in both cases: this MUST overflow.
2748 // Create a simple add instruction, and insert it into the struct.
2749 return OverflowResult::AlwaysOverflows;
2750 }
2751
2752 if (LHSKnownNonNegative && RHSKnownNonNegative) {
2753 // The sign bit is clear in both cases: this CANNOT overflow.
2754 // Create a simple add instruction, and insert it into the struct.
2755 return OverflowResult::NeverOverflows;
2756 }
2757 }
2758
2759 return OverflowResult::MayOverflow;
2760}
